title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: This is exactly why false alarms are so dangerous. Overly sensitive alarms are deadly, and intentionally setting off alarms needs to be heavily punished because of the repercussions.
author: @toblexson5020

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: My wife once lived on the top floor of a >10-story dorm that kept having its fire alarms pulled. Every time, she'd have to walk down about a dozen floors to evacuate. I remember one time, shortly before they caught someone pulling an alarm, she mused about just hiding out in her closet instead. She's a responsible person, but everyone has their limits when it comes to false alarms. I definitely did think of this when she said that, though.
author: @serisothikos

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: 3 dead, 5 severely injured, 51 "normal" injured. And what do you get? 3 years in prison. Utter bull shit. I hate drunk idiots.
author: @alfadasfire

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: I will never understand the mindset that it's funny, comical or "just a prank" to pull fire alarms when there is no fire and certainly not a prank to set a fire. What makes this even more egregious, the light, then reduced sentence these two received and served. They killed and maimed out of sheer idiocy and were definitely old and experienced enough to know the difference. The on camera interview lie clearly reveals this.
author: @JamesSullivan-ru4op

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: Doing 3 years for murdering 3 young men and maiming others severely for life is absolute madness. Don’t know how they got away with that
author: @Kennyperse

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body:
Deliberately setting a fire, leaving instead of monitoring it and then voluntarily going on TV to lie about it after it gets out of control is just complete psycho behaviour.

These dudes are deeply disturbed and belong in a dark cell or a psych ward, not in society.
author: @HamEggsButteredToast

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: Their prison sentence is a joke. Only serving 3 years for killing 3 and maiming countless others is such a disservice to those who died
author: @melissaq8854

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body:
I’m an alumnus of Seton Hall University (yes, I’m sure he’s seen the corrections already) from about ten years ago. The Boland Hall fire left its mark to this day. Every building, not just residence halls, takes fire safety EXTREMELY seriously. Not just freshmen, but every year of student is reminded about Boland Hall and why all fire drills need to be followed. The school is also extremely strict on furnishings that students bring in (e.g. no flammable lampshades).

Thank you for covering this topic!
author: @Dragonchick27

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: "I Was young, drunk, and stupid" does not qualify as an excuse for several cases of at least aggravated manslaughter and injury to many others. If you're in college you're adult enough and intelligent enough to understand that your actions have consequences. Had I been the Judge these two would have gotten the maximum sentences possible without parole to show other college students that we expect responsible behavior from them in society.
author: @P_RO_

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: If you have ever seen a sofa catch fire, it is terrifying. We set fire to one at a bonfire once, and the sofa had 20 foot (6 meter) tall flames in less than a minute.  The materials used in most sofas are highly flammable and give off toxic fumes. A sofa fire is no joke.
author: @spacecadet35

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: Why pull the manual alarm, or buy a smoke generator, when you can actually set fire to some furnishings instead?
author: @JonosBtheMC

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body:
Those 2 dudes are assholes.
Also how they call starting a fire a prank when it's just lighting a banner with other flammable shit on the wall?
That's damn arson, firebugs or whatever they hell it is; Australia has instances of idiots doing that during bushfire season and they just light a fire for kicks and shits.
author: @RoboP

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: I used to live in an apartment building that had a "no-smoking" policy. However, several neighbors routinely smoked weed, setting off the fire alarms in the middle of the night. I begged management to evict those neighbors, because they were normalizing all the other residents to hearing the fire alarms and not evacuating. Management said they couldn't doing anything if there wasn't any proof that the neighbors were smoking. Apparently the smell of marijuana and a screaming fire alarm weren't enough. I moved out as soon as I got the chance, because all of those units had natural gas stovetops and ovens, and I knew it was just a matter of time until a real fire started and no one would bother to leave their apartments until it was too late. 😢
author: @kentslocum

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body:
Honestly, when I was attending university, there were several times when the fire alarm went off. However none of them were pranks and were often set off by hair spray or students not knowing how to use a microwave.
In one case, the alarm was pulled because construction workers digging right next to my dorm building had accidentally punctured a gas line.
In every case, students never took chances and left immediately, making sure that their roommates and neighbors also evacuated.
author: @rapidthrash1964

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: The 2 guys who caused the deadly fire? They got to live well into adulthood phase, got married and have a family of their own while being able to have LAWYERS to speak on their behalf because they REFUSE to talk to the press about their current lives, admit and honestly apologize for their grave "mistakes". How in the world these perpetrators can sleep at night peacefully after causing 3 deaths and serious fire burn injuries to the rest of other students in Boland Hall?

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: Never take any chances if you suspect a fire. In 911 NYC and Grenfell Tower London people were told to remain in place by authorities, only for both structures to turn into infernos

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: I went to university in NJ like 9 years after this incident (we also had an arson on campus funnily enough) and they took fire safety so seriously that they made us watch the entirety of the Station nightclub footage during our first night orientation. Going from that horrible graphic video of people dying, immediately into doing new student ice breakers was… a choice
author: @normslackdonald

title: This Prank Killed Three Students: The Boland Hall Fire | Fascinating Horror
body: This is something I teach my family everytime again I feel it's necessary: NEVER ignore a fire alarm. It's better to suffer another inconvenience (i.e. getting out of bed in the middle of the night and out in the cold) than to burn alive. I also tell them to learn the emergency ways and exits when we stay in a hotel.

title: WHAT I WISH I KNEW BEFORE GOING TO SHU (Seton Hall University+ tips for freshman)
body: My son just transferred after his first semester.   The Seton Hall in the brochures and the tours was nothing like the reality with nothing to do on campus on the weekends.  Many clubs are listed but are inactive.  If you can find a club that interests you, many clubs met during class time which made it hard to join groups.  His roommate was a great kid and he had met some good friends but with nothing to do on campus and no car, it wasn't very enjoyable.

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: I can still remember my first day of class at Rutgers, and getting scared off by all of the people boarding the EE bus at the bus stop by College Hall on Douglass. That plus a bad roommate situation in New Gibbons (I was at Douglass College…that was before the colleges merged) caused me to originally withdraw from the school. After my father forced me to go back and get reinstated, I started fresh the following semester living in Jameson and getting involved with a community service fraternity called Alpha Phi Omega (they’re still active on campus). It gave me a sense of community and made a huge university feel a little smaller. I eventually graduated with a Psychology major and a music minor.
author: @dorothysewing9997

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: Great video, Emily! I definitely can understand your situation! I too was unhappy and uncomfortable with my initial undergraduate school that I attended. I decided to leave because it was just too small for my liking. It really wasn't for me. I then transferred to Arizona State University and it was the best decision that I ever made. I graduated Summa Cum Laude and as a result, landed a terrific job. Sometimes,  experiencing something that you ultimately find isn't for you, points you to what you really want. There's nothing wrong with this. It's part of life! Glad that you found what is best for you! What's ironic though, is that I'll be attending graduate school at Rutgers this fall! Be happy and I love your videos!!
author: @donwest3342

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: Just the thought of going to college for $40,000+ in tuition per year really freaks me out. Private or not, the amount of money that colleges charge people is exploitive not to mention, it keeps going up! I mean, can you imagine having it in the back of your mind that you have to pay back all of that money now during covid where so many people are not working, can barely put food on the table much less can pay their rent/mortgage??  That said, when I go to college I am going to know EXACTLY what I want even if it means that I end up going a year or two later than everybody else.

She has a degree in the Classics. She thought Rutgers was a second rate school. Furthermore, she then went on for a degree in linguistics and a law degree. You're an intelligent young woman.
